message:
feat(btree): extract rowid from index key records for IdxRowid opcode

Implement proper index cursor rowid extraction where the rowid is stored
as the trailing integer field in the serialized key record:

- cursor.rs: Update rowid() to parse the cell payload as a record and
  extract the last field as an integer when cell.rowid is None (index
  cursor path). Add comprehensive tests covering normal index keys,
  overflow payloads, and error cases for malformed records.

- engine.rs: Wire IdxRowid opcode to actually extract the rowid from
  the index cursor via cursor_rowid() instead of being a no-op. This
  is essential for queries that need to look up table rows after
  finding matches in an index.

- Minor formatting fixes in connection.rs and codegen.rs

This completes the index maintenance chain: INSERT now emits IdxInsert
to add index entries, and SELECT can now use IdxRowid to retrieve the
table rowid for fetching the full row data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
